compiled_state.title: Add hyperfine install step to CI workflow for baseline/regression benchmarking
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Task

Add a hyperfine install step to .github/workflows/ci.yml so the regression suite (future Story 52f9-be06) and any baseline re-captures can run in CI.

### Steps

1. Edit .github/workflows/ci.yml: in the test job (or a new benchmark job, whichever matches where the regression suite will run), add 'sudo apt-get install -y hyperfine' as a step before any capture-baseline.sh or regression-suite invocation.
2. Confirm the install works on ubuntu-latest (package is in the default Ubuntu archive for 22.04+; no PPA needed).
3. No regression tests yet — this is infrastructure prep for Story G (52f9-be06).

### Done Definition

- .github/workflows/ci.yml has a 'Install hyperfine' step
- A dry-run invocation 'hyperfine --version' is added to verify install (failing the job if install failed)
- RED test: tests/scripts/test-ci-hyperfine-installed.sh greps the workflow file for the install step

### Why

Epic 78fc-3858 SC6 (regression gate) and SC7 (≥60% aggregate reduction) require benchmark infra that runs in CI. Installing hyperfine at this point establishes the dependency cleanly rather than as a last-minute fix in Story G.

comments.body: CHECKPOINT: test written (RED->GREEN), ci.yml edited. test-ci-hyperfine-installed.sh: PASSED 3/3. .test-index updated. Ready for commit workflow.
comments.author: Test
